pattern: A breadth-first search over an eight-connected grid of open cells finds the fewest cells on a clear corner-to-corner path.
hints: Shortest number of steps on an unweighted grid is exactly what a breadth-first search measures.
Cells are walkable only when they hold 0, and movement includes the four diagonals as well as the four orthogonal directions.
BFS from (0,0) counting visited cells, expand to all eight neighbors that are in-bounds and 0, and return the count when you first reach the bottom-right corner, or -1 if it is unreachable.

edge_inputs.description: fully open grid where the diagonal is shortest

stress.description: random grids with a clear start and end, varied blockage density
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 60))
    grid = [[1 if rng.random() < 0.3 else 0 for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(n)]
    grid[0][0] = 0
    grid[n - 1][n - 1] = 0
    return [grid]
stress.note: endpoints forced open; path may or may not exist
